steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Use the angle sum property of triangles
steps.content:
The angles in any triangle sum to 180°. We have angle A = 54°, angle B = 90°, and angle C = (k/2)°.

Setting up the equation:
$$54 + 90 + \frac{k}{2} = 180$$
Simplifying the left side:
$$144 + \frac{k}{2} = 180$$
Isolating the term with k:
$$\frac{k}{2} = 36$$

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content:
From $\frac{k}{2} = 36$, multiply both sides by 2:
$$k = 72$$
That confirms C is correct.
